message:
Render BottomSheet via Portal to escape parent transform stacking contexts

Root cause: The BottomSheet rendered inside the component tree where
parent elements (FrankenContainer, FrankenGlitch) apply CSS transforms
and filters. CSS transforms create new stacking contexts, which trap
position:fixed children — the bottom sheet would render behind or
clipped by its parent's overflow, instead of floating above the page.

Fix: Added a Portal component that uses React's createPortal to
render the sheet directly on document.body, completely escaping
all parent stacking contexts. The Portal defers rendering until
after mount (useState + useEffect) to avoid SSR hydration mismatches.

Visual redesign of the sheet chrome:
  - Header: Binary icon + title in font-black uppercase tracking,
    larger close button (h-12 w-12 rounded-full) with X icon that
    rotates 90 degrees on hover
  - Content area: custom-scrollbar with green-themed webkit scrollbar
    (4px wide, green-500/20 thumb), max-w-4xl centered content
  - Footer: decorative "Lexicon_Protocol_Secure" status indicator
    with 8-segment bar visualization
  - Backdrop: darker (bg-black/80) with stronger blur (backdrop-blur-md)
  - Sheet: taller max-height (92vh vs 85vh), deeper rounded corners
    (rounded-t-[2.5rem]), spring animation gains mass:0.8 parameter
    for slightly heavier feel
  - z-index: fixed at z-[999] on the Portal wrapper instead of
    relying on CSS custom properties that could be overridden

New imports: useState, createPortal, cn, X icon, Binary icon.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
